data.title: New York Times Shareholders Back Board, Pay and Auditors
data.description:

New York Times ( (NYT) ) just unveiled an update.

At its annual meeting of stockholders held on April 22, 2026, The New York Times Company’s shareholders elected all management-nominated directors, with Class A and Class B common stockholders voting separately. The vote reaffirmed the existing board structure, including both independent directors and Class B representatives aligned with the controlling family interests.

Shareholders also ratified the selection of Ernst & Young LLP as the Company’s independent auditors for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2026, signaling continued confidence in its external financial oversight. In addition, Class B common stockholders approved, on an advisory basis, the compensation of the Company’s named executive officers, indicating support from the controlling shareholder group for current executive pay practices.

The most recent analyst rating on (NYT) stock is a Hold with a $84.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on New York Times stock, see the NYT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NYT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NYT is a Outperform.

The score is driven mainly by strong financial performance (margin expansion, strong free cash flow, and a debt-free balance sheet) and supportive earnings-call guidance pointing to continued growth and capital returns. Offsetting factors are a stretched technical setup (overbought signals) and a rich valuation (high P/E with modest yield).

To see Spark’s full report on NYT stock, click here.

More about New York Times

The New York Times Company is a media and publishing company focused on news, opinion and multimedia journalism, primarily through its flagship newspaper, digital news platforms and related products. It operates in the news and information industry, targeting global readers, advertisers and subscribers with premium, subscription-based content and services.

On April 23, 2026, BioRestorative Therapies, Inc. released an updated corporate presentation intended for use at conferences and investor meetings, making it available on its website. The company emphasized that the information is being furnished rather than filed, meaning it is not automatically subject to certain Exchange Act liabilities or incorporated into its other securities filings unless specifically referenced.

The move signals an effort to provide investors and stakeholders with refreshed corporate information without formally designating the material as complete or material for investment decisions. By structuring the disclosure this way, BioRestorative retains flexibility around the legal status of the presentation while still updating the market on its narrative and positioning.

data.title: Vaxart Adds Veteran Biotech Leader to Board
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Vaxart ( (VXRT) ).

On April 23, 2026, Vaxart, Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing oral vaccines using a proprietary pill-based delivery platform, said it had appointed veteran biotech executive James B. Breitmeyer, M.D., Ph.D., to its Board of Directors. Vaxart’s pipeline targets coronavirus, norovirus, influenza and a therapeutic HPV indication, with vaccines designed for room-temperature storage and needle-free administration.

The appointment brings more than three decades of clinical development and regulatory experience to Vaxart’s board, including leadership roles at Oncternal Therapeutics, Bavarian Nordic, Eli Lilly and other biopharma companies, where Breitmeyer contributed to multiple FDA approvals and vaccine programs. Vaxart’s directors said his background in drug and vaccine development is expected to reinforce oversight of its clinical programs and support advancement of its oral vaccine platform as the company works to validate the benefits of its technology for patients and investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (VXRT) stock is a Buy with a $4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Vaxart stock, see the VXRT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on VXRT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VXRT is a Neutral.

The score is driven mainly by the improved TTM financial profile (rapid revenue growth and a return to profitability/positive cash flow), tempered by concerns about sustainability given historically volatile results and a smaller equity cushion. Technicals are supportive with the stock above key moving averages, while valuation looks inexpensive on P/E but lacks dividend support and relies on potentially non-recurring earnings. Earnings call factors are constructive due to partnership validation and extended runway, but remain risk-weighted due to BARDA-related delays and partner-dependency.

To see Spark’s full report on VXRT stock, click here.

More about Vaxart

Vaxart, Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing oral recombinant vaccines using a proprietary pill-based delivery platform. Its vaccines are designed to be stored and shipped without refrigeration and target indications including coronavirus, norovirus, influenza and a therapeutic human papillomavirus vaccine, supported by broad domestic and international patent filings.

The company aims to create oral versions of currently marketed vaccines and new recombinant vaccines, positioning itself in the vaccine market as a needle-free, more easily distributed alternative. By focusing on adenovirus- and TLR3 agonist-based technologies, Vaxart is pursuing both infectious disease prevention and immune-oncology applications, with a particular emphasis on global accessibility and simplified vaccination logistics.

Average Trading Volume: 491,637

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $182.8M

The payment, equal to R$0.45453469202 per common share, underscores a significant cash return to investors and signals the company’s capacity to remunerate shareholders while maintaining compliance with Brazilian corporate and market regulations.

data.title: Mawson Infrastructure Appeals Nasdaq Delisting Amid Rebranding Plan
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mawson Infrastructure Group ( (MIGI) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 17, 2026, Mawson Infrastructure Group received a Nasdaq delisting determination after its reported stockholders’ equity fell below the $2.5 million requirement as of December 31, 2025. The company intends to request a hearing before a Nasdaq panel, a move that will stay any suspension or delisting while it presents its case that it now meets the equity standard.

In a parallel step, the company is rebranding as Big Digital Energy, Inc., with the legal name change in Delaware effective April 24, 2026, and its Nasdaq ticker expected to switch from MIGI to BGDE on April 30, 2026. The rebrand, accompanied by a new corporate website, is pitched by management as a strategic pivot toward energy-focused digital infrastructure, but the pending Nasdaq review keeps regulatory and listing risks in focus for shareholders.

Chief executive Phil Stanley framed the delisting notice as a legacy issue of prior management and asserted that the company’s current financial position and operational trajectory support ongoing compliance. For investors, the combination of a fresh corporate identity, unchanged CUSIP, and an active appeal process suggests continuity of trading in the near term, while highlighting the importance of the Nasdaq panel’s eventual decision for the firm’s market standing.

Spark’s Take on MIGI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MIGI is a Neutral.

The score is driven down primarily by weak financial performance (persistent losses, negative equity, and renewed cash burn). Technicals provide some near-term support due to price strength versus moving averages, but overbought signals and high volatility elevate risk. Corporate events are mixed, with strategic repositioning offset by governance/legal turbulence, while valuation is constrained by negative earnings and no dividend.

To see Spark’s full report on MIGI stock, click here.

More about Mawson Infrastructure Group

Mawson Infrastructure Group Inc., set to be renamed Big Digital Energy, Inc., is a U.S.-based technology company that designs, builds, and operates next-generation digital infrastructure platforms. It provides artificial intelligence and high-performance computing services, alongside Bitcoin and other digital asset mining, through both self-mining and colocation models, emphasizing carbon-free energy sources such as nuclear power.

With 129 megawatts of capacity already online and additional capacity under development, the company positions itself as a competitive provider of carbon-aware digital infrastructure solutions. Its vertically integrated approach targets scalability and efficiency for enterprise customers that require intensive compute applications and environmentally sustainable power.

The company is currently listed on Nasdaq under the ticker MIGI, with plans to transition to BGDE as part of its broader rebranding. Its strategy focuses on serving the growing digital economy’s computing needs while seeking to maintain exchange listing compliance and access to capital markets for continued infrastructure expansion.

Average Trading Volume: 547,200

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $35.06M

Find detailed analytics on MIGI stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Outlook Therapeutics Announces New Equity Financing Transaction
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Outlook Therapeutics ( (OTLK) ) has issued an update.

On April 22, 2026, Outlook Therapeutics entered into a securities purchase agreement with institutional investors for a registered direct offering of 16,129,033 common shares and a concurrent private placement of an equal number of common warrants, both priced at $0.31 per share-warrant unit. The offering, which closed on April 23, 2026 and generated approximately $5 million in gross proceeds before fees, is intended primarily to support working capital and general corporate purposes, while also requiring future shareholder approval to increase authorized shares and permit warrant exercises.

The structure of the deal gives investors significant warrant coverage and embeds ownership caps to limit any single holder’s beneficial stake, while Outlook agreed to extensive fees and additional warrants for its placement agent, H.C. Wainwright & Co. In connection with the transaction, the company also amended older warrants issued in January 2025 to reduce their exercise price to $0.31 and extend their exercisability, a move that may facilitate additional future equity inflows but also signals continued reliance on dilutive financings to fund operations.

The most recent analyst rating on (OTLK) stock is a Hold with a $0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Outlook Therapeutics stock, see the OTLK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on OTLK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, OTLK is a Neutral.

The score is primarily constrained by very weak financial performance (large losses, negative equity, and heavy cash burn), reinforcing high funding and dilution risk. Technicals remain in a broader downtrend despite some near-term stabilization, while valuation offers limited support due to ongoing unprofitability. Corporate events add near-term risk (dilution, refinancing constraints, Nasdaq compliance), partially offset by continued FDA engagement on ONS-5010.

To see Spark’s full report on OTLK stock, click here.

More about Outlook Therapeutics

Outlook Therapeutics, Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing therapies in the ophthalmology space, particularly for retinal diseases. Its primary product efforts center on injectable biologics targeting eye conditions, positioning the company within the specialty pharma and biotechnology segment of the healthcare market.

data.title: Amerant Bancorp Highlights Strong First-Quarter 2026 Performance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Amerant Bancorp ( (AMTB) ) is now available.

Amerant Bancorp reported that in the first quarter of 2026 it generated net income of $17.9 million, or $0.44 per diluted share, up sharply from $2.7 million, or $0.07 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2025, driven by improved efficiency and portfolio optimization. The quarter saw modest growth in assets, loans and deposits, lower noninterest expense, a stronger efficiency ratio and higher returns on assets and equity, alongside continued credit risk management efforts that included reductions in classified loans but a slight rise in non-performing assets.

Amerant’s balance sheet shifts included higher investment securities and core deposits, reduced cash and cash equivalents, and a loan-to-deposit ratio that edged down to 85.07%, while net interest income and margin declined amid a lower average loan yield and steady deposit costs. The board also reinforced shareholder returns by repurchasing about 859,000 Class A shares for roughly $18.7 million and declaring a $0.09 per-share cash dividend on common stock payable on May 29, 2026, to shareholders of record on May 15, 2026.

To discuss its first-quarter 2026 performance and strategic progress, Amerant scheduled a live earnings webcast and conference call for April 24, 2026, with supporting materials available through its investor relations website, underscoring a focus on transparency with investors. Management emphasized the bank’s strong fundamentals, cost-saving initiatives and disciplined credit posture as key to supporting long-term growth, stability and value creation for customers, communities and shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (AMTB) stock is a Hold with a $23.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Amerant Bancorp stock, see the AMTB Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on AMTB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AMTB is a Neutral.

AMTB scores 56 driven primarily by an improving but still uneven financial profile and a cautious earnings-call backdrop highlighting profitability, expense, and asset-quality headwinds. Technicals are mixed with limited near-term momentum, while valuation is reasonable and modestly supportive.

To see Spark’s full report on AMTB stock, click here.

More about Amerant Bancorp

Amerant Bancorp Inc. is a bank holding company headquartered in Coral Gables, Florida, operating primarily through Amerant Bank, N.A. and Amerant Investments, Inc. The company provides deposit, credit and wealth management services to individuals and businesses, supported by a network of 23 banking centers across South Florida and Tampa, reflecting its regional focus in the Florida banking market.

Average Trading Volume: 279,394

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $947.8M

For a thorough assessment of AMTB stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Customers Bancorp Posts Strong Q1 2026 Results, Growth
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Customers Bancorp ( (CUBI) ) has provided an update.

Customers Bancorp reported strong first-quarter 2026 results on April 23, 2026, with net income available to common shareholders of $69.7 million, or $1.97 per diluted share, and core earnings essentially in line. The bank delivered ROAA of 1.13%, ROCE of about 13%, an efficiency ratio below 50%, and year-over-year gains in revenue, core earnings, and book value per share of 58%, 28% and 16%, respectively.

Loans and leases held for investment rose 3.7% quarter-over-quarter and 15.3% year-over-year to $17.4 billion, driven by specialized C&I, mortgage finance, and commercial real estate segments, while investment securities decreased modestly. Deposits grew 3.9% from the prior quarter and 14.0% year-over-year to $21.6 billion, with a lower average cost and record non-interest-bearing balances, as capital and tangible common equity climbed to $2.1 billion and asset quality remained strong with NPAs at 0.29% of total assets and reserves at 337% of non-performing loans.

The quarter also marked the completion of a planned leadership transition effective January 1, 2026, with Sam Sidhu succeeding Jay Sidhu as CEO and emphasizing disciplined growth in structure and credit quality. The company increased borrowings mainly through FHLB advances, enhanced liquidity coverage of uninsured deposits to roughly 151%, improved its TCE/TA ratio to 8.3%, and repurchased over 621,000 common shares, signaling confidence in its franchise strength and balance sheet resilience.

The most recent analyst rating on (CUBI) stock is a Buy with a $84.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Customers Bancorp stock, see the CUBI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CUBI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CUBI is a Outperform.

Overall score is supported by a strong, positive earnings-call outlook and favorable price momentum above key moving averages. This is tempered by only mid-level financial statement quality driven by normalized profitability versus 2021–2022 and historical cash-flow volatility, while valuation appears reasonable on an ~11x P/E.

To see Spark’s full report on CUBI stock, click here.

More about Customers Bancorp

Customers Bancorp, Inc., based in West Reading, Pa., operates as a commercial bank holding company focused on loans and leases to commercial and industrial clients, commercial real estate borrowers, and mortgage finance customers. The bank emphasizes specialized C&I lending, multifamily and owner-occupied CRE, and maintains a significant base of non-interest-bearing and digitally sourced deposits.

Average Trading Volume: 406,678

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $2.63B

A10 Networks ( (ATEN) ) just unveiled an update.

A10 Networks, Inc. held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders on April 22, 2026, with 92.56% of eligible shares represented in person or by proxy. Stockholders elected five directors, including Tor R. Braham, Peter Y. Chung, Eric Singer, Dhrupad Trivedi and Dana Wolf, to serve until the 2027 annual meeting, reflecting broad support for the board slate.

Investors also approved, on a non-binding basis, the company’s executive compensation program and backed holding say-on-pay advisory votes on an annual schedule. In addition, stockholders ratified the appointment of Grant Thornton LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2026, reinforcing continuity in A10 Networks’ governance and oversight structures.
